name: Pic de Tristagne and Étang Fourcat Loop Trail
name_original: Pic de Tristagne – boucle trail au départ du parking de Pradieres d'en haut – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
description: This difficult 13.8-mile (22.2 km) jogging loop to Pic de Tristagne offers rugged alpine terrain and panoramic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Auzat are set within the Regional Natural Park of Ariège Pyrenees, offering diverse and preserved natural environments. The region features significant elevation changes, with altitudes ranging from 720m to 3140m, providing varied gradients for runners. Joggers can explore extensive trail networks, including sections of long-distance paths, through mountainous terrain and the scenic Vicdessos valley. The landscape combines rocky escarpments with green areas, alongside lakes and rivers. Best jogging routes around Auzat The most popular jogging route is Le Pic de Montcalm – trail route starting from l'Artigue – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises, an 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route ascends into the high mountain area of the Pyrenees Ariégeoises. Another top favourite among local runners is Pic de Tristagne and Étang Fourcat Loop Trail, a difficult 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. This route features mountain peaks and high-altitude lakes. Local runners also love the Burgruine und Grotten – Auzat loop from Auzat, a 15.4 miles (24.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Jogging around Auzat is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive trail networks, and the picturesque Vicdessos Valley. The network offers options for both challenging mountain runs and more moderate trails. The routes in Auzat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 5 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Auzat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Auzat?
A: Auzat offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, from challenging mountain runs to more moderate paths within the scenic Vicdessos Valley.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running trails in Auzat?
A: While Auzat is known for its mountainous terrain and challenging routes, the region also offers options for less experienced runners. The extensive trail networks include paths with varied gradients, though many routes feature significant elevation changes. Currently, there are no routes explicitly categorized as 'easy' in our selection, but some trails within the Vicdessos Valley may offer a more moderate experience.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Auzat area?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes around Auzat. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/28934385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgruine und Grotten – Auzat loop from Auzat&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 15.4-mile trail that allows you to start and finish in the same location, offering varied terrain.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails near Auzat?
A: Jogging routes around Auzat offer incredibly diverse and preserved natural scenery. You can expect breathtaking views of the Pyrenees, the picturesque Vicdessos Valley, rocky escarpments, and lush green areas. Many trails also pass by serene lakes and rivers, providing a tranquil backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or natural features to look out for while running?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in natural beauty. While running, you might encounter stunning high-altitude lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang Fourcat&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974171629/pic-de-tristagne-and-etang-fourcat-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Tristagne and Étang Fourcat Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features sites like the Cirque d'Anglade and Cirque du Sisca, offering dramatic mountain vistas.

Q: Are the running routes around Auzat suitable for families?
A: Auzat's natural environment offers a variety of trails, some of which are more suitable for families looking for a quieter, greener atmosphere. However, given the significant elevation changes and mountainous terrain, many routes are quite challenging. It's best to check the difficulty and distance of specific routes to ensure they align with your family's fitness levels.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the jogging trails in Auzat?
A: Auzat is located within the Regional Natural Park of Ariège Pyrenees, which generally welcomes dogs on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash to protect wildlife and livestock. It's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage, especially in protected areas or near nature reserves, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Auzat?
A: The best time to go running in Auzat is typically during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. During these periods, the weather is generally more favorable, and the trails are more accessible. Winter can bring snow and ice, especially at higher altitudes, making many routes challenging or impassable for jogging.

Q: Where can I park when accessing the running trails in Auzat?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the specific trailhead. Many popular trail starting points, especially those leading to well-known routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974169019/le-pic-de-montcalm-trail-route-starting-from-l-artigue-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Pic de Montcalm trail&lt;/a&gt; from l'Artigue, usually have designated parking areas. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific parking information before you head out.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Auzat?
A: The running routes in Auzat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 5 reviews. Over 700 runners have used komoot to explore Auzat's varied terrain, often praising the challenging mountainous runs, extensive trail networks, and the picturesque Vicdessos Valley.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or rivers?
A: Yes, the Auzat region is dotted with beautiful lakes and rivers, and several running routes incorporate these natural features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974171629/pic-de-tristagne-and-etang-fourcat-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic de Tristagne and Étang Fourcat Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes you past high-altitude lakes, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2831132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang Fourcat&lt;/a&gt;. The proximity to various lakes and direct access to a river from certain areas provide tranquil and scenic running opportunities.

Q: What are the typical elevation changes on running routes in Auzat?
A: Auzat is known for its significant elevation changes, with altitudes ranging from 720m to 3140m. Many running routes feature substantial ascents and descents, offering a challenging experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10537743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang d'Alate – Port de Saleix loop&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1200 meters of elevation gain, making it suitable for runners seeking a demanding workout.

Q: Can I find routes that offer a more challenging mountain running experience?
A: Absolutely. Auzat is a prime destination for challenging mountain running. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974169019/le-pic-de-montcalm-trail-route-starting-from-l-artigue-parc-naturel-regional-des-pyrenees-ariegeoises" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Pic de Montcalm – trail route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6983864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refuge de Bassiès – Pique Rouge de Bassiès loop&lt;/a&gt; ascend into high mountain areas, featuring considerable distances and significant elevation gains, perfect for experienced trail runners.
tips: Very nice loop hike made last week from the power station but watch out for the last snowfields.
The peak of Tristagne, which rises to 2,878 m above sea level, is located on the border between Andorra and France. On the Andorran side, there is the Tristagne circus and its three lakes.
The port of Albeille is a pass between the Pic de Tristagne and the Pic de l'Albeille on the border between Andorra and France.
The Izourt pond is an artificial body of water at an altitude of 1,652 meters on the Artiès stream. A small shelter is located near the dam.

faqs.question: Where does the Pic de Tristagne trail loop start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail loop for Pic de Tristagne begins from the upper Pradieres car park. This car park serves as the primary access point for the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pic de Tristagne trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is exceptionally challenging, characterized by significant elevation changes, steep ascents and descents, and rocky sections. It features highly technical, difficult, and sometimes hazardous sections, often requiring scrambling (using your hands). The Ariège region's 'raw verticality' means paths are far from smooth, and continuous jogging is generally not feasible on many parts of this route.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Pic de Tristagne route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking panoramic views, especially from the summit of Pic de Tristaina. You'll encounter several beautiful lakes, including Étang Fourcat and Refuge de l'Étang Fourcat, Etang de la Goueille, Etang de Petsiguer, and Izourt Pond. Ridge walks provide fabulous panoramas, often straddling the border between France and Andorra, and you'll pass through Port de l'Arbella and enjoy the View of the Artiès Valley.

faqs.question: Is the Pic de Tristagne trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is not suitable for beginners or families. It is described as an exceptionally challenging route, requiring very good fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy shoes, and alpine experience. The terrain is highly technical and difficult, making it unsuitable for those without significant high-mountain hiking or trail running experience.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to undertake this trail loop?
faqs.answer: The best time for difficult alpine hiking like the Pic de Tristagne loop is typically during the summer months, from late June to early September, when snow has melted from higher elevations and weather conditions are generally more stable. However, always check the local weather forecast before heading out, as mountain conditions can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Is the Pic de Tristagne trail loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises generally allows dogs, specific regulations may apply in certain areas, especially near protected wildlife zones or refuges. Given the trail's extreme difficulty and technical sections, it is not recommended for most dogs, as it could be hazardous for them and their owners.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on public trails within the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning wildlife, camping, and fire restrictions, as these can vary within the park.

faqs.question: Does the Pic de Tristagne trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails. You will find parts of the GR 10A, La traversée des Pyrénées - sec.3, HRP Étape 30, and various HRP Variante sections (30-31.1, 29.3, 29.4, 30.1, and Étape 29), as well as the Paserelle trail.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and alpine nature, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, appropriate layers of clothing for varying weather conditions, a waterproof jacket, sufficient water (as sources may be limited or unreliable), high-energy snacks, a map and compass or GPS device, a headlamp, and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles can also be very helpful on steep and rocky sections.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like refuges or water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes by Refuge de l'Étang Fourcat, which can offer a potential stop or emergency shelter. However, it's crucial to confirm its opening status and services beforehand. Water sources can be found near lakes and streams, but always filter or treat any water collected from natural sources.

faqs.question: What is the best strategy for tackling the Pic de Tristagne loop given its difficulty?
faqs.answer: Due to its length and significant elevation gain, an early start is highly recommended to allow ample time to complete the loop before nightfall. Be prepared for a long day, potentially exceeding the estimated duration if you are not accustomed to such technical alpine terrain. Carrying enough water and food is critical, as resupply options are minimal. It's also wise to check the weather forecast for the high mountains, as conditions can change rapidly and dramatically.

waypoints.name: Étang Fourcat and Refuge de l'Étang Fourcat
